vulnerabilities.description: Unencrypted local storage of potentially sensitive data. memory.json, session JSON files, and localStorage todos are stored in plain text without encryption or access control. On shared systems, other users could access this data.

Temporary files created in system tmpdir may have world-readable permissions. While filenames use random bytes, on shared systems other users could potentially read GitHub content (PR bodies, issue comments) left in temp files if permissions aren't restrictive.

No input validation on key names or values in memory helper. Arbitrary command-line arguments are accepted without sanitization. Potential for unintended data types or prototype pollution if keys like '__proto__' are passed.
Silent error handling during memory load. JSON parse errors are caught and ignored, potentially masking data corruption or loss without alerting the user.

Insufficient validation of localStorage data structure. While JSON.parse has try-catch, no schema validation ensures the loaded object matches expected todo structure (Array of objects with id/text/completed fields).

recommendations: Implement encryption for memory.json and session files using crypto module (AES-256 recommended) before writing to disk
Restrict file permissions to owner-only (0o600) for sensitive data files in .claude-flow directory
Add schema validation in memory.js to prevent prototype pollution: reject keys like '__proto__', 'constructor', 'prototype'
In github-safe.js, set restrictive permissions on temp files using fs.chmodSync(tmpFile, 0o600) immediately after creation
Add logging/warnings instead of silent error handling in memory.js line 18-20 to surface data corruption issues
Validate localStorage data structure in app.js with a schema validator to ensure todos array contains expected object shapes
Add Content Security Policy (CSP) headers to index.html to further mitigate XSS: `<meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy" content="default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'">`
Consider using IndexedDB with encryption instead of localStorage for sensitive todo data
securityStrengths: ✓ Excellent injection prevention in github-safe.js using execFileSync (no shell interpolation)
✓ XSS protection via textContent instead of innerHTML for user input in app.js
✓ Atomic file writes in session.js to prevent corruption from concurrent access
✓ No hardcoded secrets, API keys, or passwords found
✓ No SQL injection risks (client-side application, no database queries)
✓ No external npm dependencies to audit
✓ Input validation (trim) on todo text before storage
